name: sqlitedict (SQLite-backed Persistent Dict)

performance.notes: SQLite backend; autocommit mode or explicit commit; write speed depends on fsync

agent_readiness.notes: Persistent dict backed by SQLite. from sqlitedict import SqliteDict. db = SqliteDict('mydb.sqlite', autocommit=True). db['key'] = {'value': 42}. db['key']. del db['key']. 'key' in db. db.commit(). len(db). for key in db: pass. for key, value in db.items(): pass. tablename='mydict' for multiple tables in one SQLite. Serialization: pickle (default), json, msgpack. SqliteDict(':memory:') for in-memory. Thread-safe: separate connection per thread recommended. PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL for better concurrency. Custom encode/decode functions.

gotchas: autocommit=False (default): must call db.commit() or changes lost on crash
Pickle serialization is default — not human-readable; JSON option available
SQLite WAL mode recommended for concurrent reads — not default
Large values (>1MB) degrade performance — consider alternative storage
best_when: Persistent Python dict with SQLite durability (cache, memoization, state)
When pickle-based shelve is too fragile and TinyDB is too structured
Multi-table storage in single SQLite file (tablename parameter)
NLP/ML pipelines needing persistent key-value cache between runs
avoid_when: Concurrent multi-process writes without WAL mode
When full document store is needed (use TinyDB or MongoDB)
summary: sqlitedict provides a persistent Python dict backed by SQLite. SqliteDict('mydb.sqlite', autocommit=True). db['key'] = obj. Pickle/JSON serialization. tablename for multi-table. Thread-safe with separate connections. Apache-2.0 by piskvorky. Best for durable Python key-value storage and caching between runs. Must commit() if autocommit=False. WAL mode for concurrency. No type annotations.
